meta.name: Thai Green Curry (Gaeng Keow Wan)
meta.subtitle: Creamy coconut green curry with aromatic herbs, green curry paste, and tender protein + vegetables.

source.name: SSA Thai Catalog
notes: Deterministic canonical green curry recipe for SSA catalogs.
Designed for store-bought green curry paste with optional fresh boosts.
Includes chicken default with tofu/seafood/vegetarian adaptations.

summary: A fragrant Thai green curry built by frying green curry paste in coconut cream, then simmering with chicken, vegetables, and Thai basil for a bright, creamy, spicy bowl served with jasmine rice.

yield.notes: Serves 4 with rice; 3 large bowls without sides.

notes: Freezing is acceptable; coconut milk can separate slightly—reheat gently and whisk.
Flavors deepen overnight.

tasteProfile.notes: Green curry is aromatic and can be quite spicy depending on paste. Adjust heat by paste quantity and extra coconut milk.

required.label: Medium pot or Dutch oven
required.notes: A heavy pot helps prevent scorching coconut cream.

optional.label: Mortar & pestle
optional.notes: Helpful for bruising lemongrass/galangal or making a quick herb mash.

ingredients.label: Coconut milk (full-fat), 2 cans
notes: If your coconut milk separates, that’s fine—use the thick cream to fry the paste.

ingredients.label: Green curry paste
notes: Start with ~2–3 Tbsp depending on brand heat. Add more later if desired.

ingredients.label: Chicken stock (or water)
ingredients.notes: Use water if needed; stock adds depth.

ingredients.label: Fish sauce
ingredients.notes: Primary salting agent in Thai curries.

ingredients.label: Palm sugar (or light brown sugar)
ingredients.notes: Balance the heat and herbal notes; adjust to taste.

ingredients.label: Kaffir lime leaves (makrut), torn or bruised
ingredients.notes: Optional but highly recommended for authentic aroma.

ingredients.label: Thai basil leaves
ingredients.notes: Add at the end to keep it bright and perfumed.

ingredients.label: Boneless skinless chicken thighs, sliced
ingredients.notes: Thighs stay tender in simmered curries.

ingredients.label: Eggplant (Thai eggplant preferred), bite-size
ingredients.notes: If unavailable, use Japanese eggplant or zucchini.

ingredients.label: Green beans, trimmed
ingredients.notes: Keeps a nice crisp bite in curry.

ingredients.label: Zucchini, half-moons (fallback veg)
ingredients.notes: Use if Thai eggplant is unavailable.

ingredients.label: Lime juice (to finish)
ingredients.notes: Optional but recommended—brightens at the end.

steps.title: Prep protein and vegetables
instructions: Slice chicken thighs into thin bite-size pieces (about 1/2-inch strips).
Cut vegetables into curry-friendly pieces: eggplant chunks, sliced bell pepper, trimmed green beans.
Separate Thai basil leaves; keep stems out (optional).
If using kaffir lime leaves, tear or bruise them to release aroma.
checks: All ingredients are prepped before heating the pot (coconut can scorch if neglected).

steps.title: Fry green curry paste in coconut cream
instructions: Open coconut milk and spoon the thick cream portion into a pot over medium-high heat.
Add green curry paste and stir constantly until fragrant and the oil begins to separate slightly (about 2–3 minutes).
If your coconut milk has little cream, use the whole can and cook a bit longer, stirring to avoid scorching.
checks: No burnt bits on the bottom of the pot.

steps.title: Build the curry base
instructions: Whisk in the remaining coconut milk and chicken stock (or water).
Add kaffir lime leaves if using.
Bring to a gentle simmer (not a hard boil).
checks: Curry is simmering gently and evenly.

steps.title: Simmer chicken until just cooked
instructions: Add sliced chicken to the simmering curry.
Simmer gently, stirring occasionally, until chicken is cooked through and tender.
checks: Chicken pieces are opaque throughout with no raw center.

steps.title: Add vegetables and simmer until tender-crisp
instructions: Add eggplant (or zucchini), bamboo shoots, green beans, and bell pepper (if using).
Simmer gently until eggplant is tender and beans are cooked but still bright.
checks: Vegetables are not overcooked and mushy.

steps.title: Season and balance (salty-sweet) then finish with herbs
instructions: Stir in fish sauce and palm sugar. Simmer 1 minute to dissolve.
Taste and adjust: more fish sauce for salt/umami; more sugar to soften heat; a spoon of coconut milk to mellow spice.
Turn off heat and fold in Thai basil. Add a squeeze of lime if using.
checks: Flavor is balanced: aromatic, slightly sweet, savory, with controllable heat.
Thai basil is bright and fragrant (not overcooked).

steps.title: Serve
instructions: Serve hot over jasmine rice.
Garnish with cilantro if desired.
Optional: offer extra lime wedges and chili flakes on the side.
checks: Curry is served hot and aromatic.

pairings.notes: Serve with plain rice to let the curry shine; crisp salads help cut richness.

leftovers.notes: Reheat gently; whisk if coconut separates. Add basil fresh if possible.

fridge.notes: Cool quickly, store airtight. Reheat gently to avoid breaking coconut.

freezer.notes: Freeze in portions. Thaw overnight; reheat gently and whisk.

notes: Cool leftovers quickly and refrigerate within 2 hours.
Ensure chicken reaches safe doneness; simmer gently until fully cooked.

adaptations.title: Adjusting for curry paste brands
notes: Paste heat and salt vary widely. Start smaller, then add more after the first simmer.
If curry tastes harsh, simmer a few extra minutes and add more coconut milk.
If curry is flat, add fish sauce (salty/umami) and a touch more sugar (roundness).

adaptations.title: No kaffir lime leaves
notes: Use lime zest (microplane) added at the end plus extra Thai basil.
Do not add too much lime juice early; it can dull coconut aroma.

ingredient.chicken_thigh.notes: Use 400–500g tofu. Pan-sear cubes first for best texture, then add near the end.

ingredient.fish_sauce.notes: Season to taste; keep the salty-sweet balance.

ingredient.shrimp.label: Shrimp, peeled and deveined
ingredient.shrimp.notes: Add in the last 3–4 minutes; cook just until opaque.

notes: Cook shrimp at the end to prevent toughness.
You can replace chicken entirely or do half chicken/half shrimp.

ingredient.green_curry_paste.notes: Start with less paste; add more only if desired.

notes: Add extra coconut milk (up to 1 additional can) for a gentler curry.
The signature move: fry curry paste in coconut cream before adding liquids.
Thai basil is best added off-heat to stay aromatic and green.
Vegetables can vary; keep a mix of tender (eggplant) and crisp (beans/pepper).

aliases: แกงเขียวหวาน

reviewNotes: All ingredients include id+label and amount value/unit (or optional flagged).
All steps include id+title and instructions[] with timers where useful.
